title: BLUELIGHT RAT: C2 via Microsoft Graph API
description: Detects non-standard processes connecting to Microsoft Graph API endpoints. BLUELIGHT uses Microsoft Graph API and OneDrive for command-and-control, blending with legitimate Microsoft traffic to evade network detection.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and (('Event ID' = '3' and ('Destination Hostname' like '*graph.microsoft.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*login.microsoftonline.com*')) and not (('Process Name' like '*\\OneDrive.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\Teams.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\OUTLOOK.EXE' or 'Process Name' like '*\\msedge.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\chrome.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\firefox.exe'))) | stats count by 'Process Name', 'Destination Hostname', 'Destination Port', 'Host Name (Server)'
falsepositives: Custom applications legitimately using Microsoft Graph API
PowerShell scripts using Graph API for administration
splunk_original: index=sysmon EventCode=3 DestinationHostname IN ("graph.microsoft.com","login.microsoftonline.com") NOT Image IN ("*onedrive.exe","*teams.exe","*outlook.exe","*msedge.exe") | stats count by Image, DestinationHostname, DestinationPort
